questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Die A has four red faces and two white faces, die B has two red and four white faces. A fair coin is tossed. If it shows head, then die $A$ is rolled thrice, otherwise die B is rolled thrice. If the first two throws of die shows red face, then the probability of getting red face in third throw is $\frac{\mathrm{a}}{\mathrm{b}}$ (in lowest terms) then $(a-1)^{b-3}$ is equal to $\qquad$
questions.solution:
$A \rightarrow$ event that die $A$ was used in first two throws
$B \rightarrow$ event that die $B$ was used in first two throws
$C \rightarrow$ event that next throws gives red face
$\mathrm{E} \rightarrow$ event that first two throws give red faces
$\begin{aligned}
& P\left(\frac{A}{E}\right)=\frac{\frac{1}{2} \times\left(\frac{4}{6}\right)^2}{\frac{1}{2} \times\left(\frac{4}{6}\right)^2+\frac{1}{2} \times\left(\frac{2}{6}\right)^2}=\frac{4}{5} \\
& P\left(\frac{B}{E}\right)=\frac{1}{5} \Rightarrow P\left(\frac{C}{E}\right)=\frac{3}{5} \\
& a=3, b=5
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Die A has 2 white and 4 red faces whereas dic B has 4 white and 2 red faces. A coin is flipped once. If it shows a head, the game continues by throwing die A , if it shows tail, then die B is to be used. If the probability that die A is used is $\frac{32}{33}$ when it is given that red turns up every time in first n throws. Then find the value of n $\qquad$
questions.solution:
$P\left(\frac{R}{A}\right)=\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^n, P\left(\frac{R}{B}\right)=\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^n$, in n throws
$P(R)=\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^n+\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^n$, in $n$ throws
$P\left(\frac{A}{R}\right)=\frac{P\left(\frac{R}{A}\right) P(A)}{P(R)}=\frac{\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^n}{\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^n+\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^n}=\frac{2^n}{2^n+1}=\frac{32}{33} \Rightarrow n=5$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A missile target may be at a point $P$ with probability $\frac{9}{10}$ or at a point $Q$ with probability $\frac{1}{10}$ we have 20 shells each of which can be fired either at point P or Q . Each shell may hit the target independently of the other shoot with probability $\frac{2}{3}$. Then number of shells must be fired at point P to hit any target with maximum probability is
questions.solution:
Let A be the event that target is hit
$E_1, E_2$ be the events to select target $P$ and $Q$ respectively
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { Then } P\left(E_1\right)=\frac{9}{10}, P\left(E_2\right)=\frac{1}{10} \\
& P\left(\frac{A}{E_1}\right)=1-\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^x, P\left(\frac{A}{E_2}\right)=1-\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{20-x} \text { where } x \text { number of shells used to select target } P \\
& \text { Then } P(A)=P\left(E_1\right) \cdot P\left(\frac{A}{E_1}\right)+P\left(E_2\right) \cdot P\left(\frac{A}{E_2}\right)=\frac{9}{10}\left(1-\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^x\right)+\frac{1}{10}\left(1-\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{20-x}\right) \\
& \frac{d P(A)}{d x}=\frac{9}{10} x-\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^x \times-\log _c 3+\frac{1}{10} \times\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{20-x} \times 1 \times-\log _{\mathrm{e}} 3=0 \\
& \frac{\log _c 3}{10}\left(9.3^{-x}-3^{x-20}\right)=0 \\
& 3^{2-x}=3^{x-20} \cdot x=11
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Two dice are rolled and at the same time two coins are tossed. Given that one of the dice had shown a six and one of the coins has shown a heads, the probability that others will also show the same combination is $\frac{p}{33}$, then $p$ is equal to $\qquad$
questions.solution:
$3 \times 11=33$

3 for $\mathrm{HH}, \mathrm{HT}$ and TH and 11 for the cases when atleast one 6 appears
So, required probability will be $\frac{1}{33}$ (as there is only one way in which the required condition is satisfied)

questions.q: $f$ is any function from $A$ to $A$ where $A=\{1,2, \ldots 10\}$. The discrete points on the graph of $f(x)$ are joined with line segments the probability that f is onto and the resulting graph has only one local minima and no local maxima is $\frac{2^{\mathrm{k}}-2}{10^{10}}$ then k is
questions.solution: Total number of function satisfying the given condition is ${ }^9 \mathrm{C}_1+{ }^9 \mathrm{C}_2+\ldots{ }^9 \mathrm{C}_8=2^9-2$ $\Rightarrow$ Probability $=\frac{2^9-2}{10^{10}}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A letter is known to have either from "TATA NAGAR" or from "CALCUTTA". On the envelope, just two consecutive letters TA are visible. If the probability that the letter came from 'TATA NAGAR" is in the form $\frac{p}{q}$, where $p$ and $q$ are co-prime positive integers, then find the value of $(p+q)$.
questions.solution:
Let $A$ be the event that the letter is from TATANAGAR and $B$ be the event that letter is from CALCUTTA.
Also, let $E$ be the event that on the letter, two consecutive letters TA are visible.
$$
P(A)=\frac{1}{2} ; \quad P(B)=\frac{1}{2}
$$

And $P(E / A)=\frac{2}{8}$ and $P(E / B)=\frac{1}{7}$
[If the letter is TATANAGAR, we see that the events of two consecutive letters visible are TA, AT, TA, AN, NA, AG, GA, AR]
Sô, $\quad P(E / A)=\frac{2}{8}$ and sāmé in cãsè of CALCUTTA, soo $P(E / B)=\frac{1}{7}$
Therefore, $P(A / E)=\frac{P(A) P(E / A)}{P(A) P(E / A)+P(B) P(E / B)}$
$$
=\frac{(1 / 2)(2 / 8)}{[(1 / 2)(2 / 8)]+[(1 / 2)(1 / 7)]}=\frac{(1 / 8)}{(1 / 8)+(1 / 14)}=\frac{(1 / 8)}{(11 / 56)}=\frac{7}{11}
$$
$p=7, q=11, p+q=11+7=18$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $E_1, E_2, E_3$ be three independent events associated with a random experiment such that $3 P\left(E_1 \cap \bar{E}_2 \cap \bar{E}_3\right)=P\left(\bar{E}_1 \cap E_2 \cap \bar{E}_3\right)=9 P\left(\bar{E}_1 \cap \bar{E}_2 \cap E_3\right)=3-3 P\left(E_1 \cup E_2 \cup E_3\right)$, where $P\left(E_1\right), P\left(E_2\right), P\left(E_3\right) \neq 1$ and $P(A)$ denotes probability of event $A$.
If absolute value of $\left|\begin{array}{lll}P\left(E_1\right) & P\left(E_2\right) & P\left(E_3\right) \\ P\left(E_2\right) & P\left(E_3\right) & P\left(E_1\right) \\ P\left(E_3\right) & P\left(E_1\right) & P\left(E_2\right)\end{array}\right|=\frac{a}{b}$, where $a, b \in N$, then find the least value of $(a+b)$.
questions.solution:
Let $P\left(E_1\right)=a, P\left(E_2\right)=b$ and $P\left(E_3\right)=c$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 3 a(1-b)(1-c)=(1-a) b(1-c)=9(1-a)(1-b) c=3(1-a)(1-b)(1-c) \\
& \frac{3 a}{1-a}=\frac{b}{1-b}=\frac{9 c}{1-c}=3 \Rightarrow a=\frac{1}{2}, b=\frac{3}{4}, c=\frac{1}{4} \\
& \text { Now, }\left|\begin{array}{lll}
1 / 2 & 3 / 4 & 1 / 4 \\
3 / 4 & 1 / 4 & 1 / 2 \\
1 / 4 & 1 / 2 & 3 / 4
\end{array}\right|=\frac{1}{64}\left|\begin{array}{lll}
2 & 3 & 1 \\
3 & 1 & 2 \\
1 & 2 & 3
\end{array}\right|=-\frac{9}{32}
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\therefore \frac{a}{b}=\frac{9}{32} \Rightarrow a+b=41
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider a family of $n$ children. Let two events $A$ and $B$ are defined as follows:
$A$ : is the event that the family has both boys and girls
$B$ : is the event that the family has atmost one girl
If the events $A$ and $B$ are independent, then find the value of $n$.
[Note: Probability that a randomly selected child is a boy or girl is same.]
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
P(A) & =\left({ }^n C_1+{ }^n C_2+{ }^n C_3+\ldots \ldots+{ }^n C_{n-1}\right)\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^n=\frac{2^n-2}{2^n} \\
P(B) & =P(0 \text { girl or 1 girl })=\left({ }^n C_0+{ }^n C_1\right)\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^n=\frac{n+1}{2^n} \\
P(A \cap B) & =P(\text { exactly one girl })={ }^n C_1 \times\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^n
\end{aligned}
$$

Now, $\quad P(A \cap B)=P(A) P(B)$
$$
\frac{n}{2^n}=\frac{2^n-2}{2^n}\left(\frac{n+1}{2^n}\right) \Rightarrow n=\frac{\left(2^n-2\right)(n+1)}{2^n}
$$

Hence, $\quad \frac{n+1}{n}=\frac{2^n}{2^n-2}=1+\frac{2}{2^n-2}$
Hence, $\quad \frac{1}{n}=\frac{2}{2^n-2}$
$$
2^n-2=2 n \Rightarrow n=3
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Mr. A either walks to school or take bus to school everyday. The probability that he takes a bus to school is $1 / 4$. If he takes a bus, the probability that he will be late is $2 / 3$. If he walks to school, the probability that he will be late is $1 / 3$. The probability that Mr. A will be on time for at least one out of two consecutive days is $\frac{p}{q}$, where $p$ and $q$ are co-prime, find the value of $(q-p)$.
questions.solution:
$A:$ Mr. A reaches late
$B_1$ : A goes to school by walking
$B_2$ : A takes but to school
$E:$ A will be on time for atleast one out of 2 consecutive days.
$$
\begin{aligned}
P\left(B_1\right) & =3 / 4 \\
P\left(B_2\right) & =1 / 4 \\
P\left(A / B_1\right) & =1 / 3 \\
P\left(A / B_2\right) & =2 / 3 \\
P(A) & =P\left(B_1 \cap A\right)+P\left(B_2 \cap A\right) \\
& =\frac{3}{4} \times \frac{1}{3}+\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{2}{3}=\frac{5}{12} \\
\therefore \quad P \quad P(E) & =1-P(A \cap A)=1-\frac{5}{12} \times \frac{5}{12}=\frac{119}{144} \equiv \frac{p}{q} \\
\Rightarrow \quad q-p & =144-119=25
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The contents of three urns are 1 white, 2 red, 3 green balls; 2 white, 1 red and 1 green balls; 4 white, 5 red and 3 green balls. Two ball are drawn from an urn chosen at random and are found to be one white and one green. If the probability that the balls so drawn came from the third urn can be expressed as $\frac{a}{b}$, where $a$ and $b$ are co-prime positive integers, find $(a+b)$.
questions.solution:
The probability of drawing one white bails and one green ball from the first urn is $\frac{1}{5}$.
The probability of drawing one white ball and one green ball from the second urn is $\frac{1}{3}$.
The probability of drawing one white ball and one green ball from the third urn is $\frac{2}{11}$.
Therefore, the probability that the third urn was chosen is $\frac{\frac{2}{11}}{\frac{1}{5}+\frac{1}{3}+\frac{2}{11}}=\frac{15}{59}=\frac{a}{b}$
Hence $15+59=74$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

If $P\left(\frac{C-B}{A}\right)=\frac{a}{b}$ where $a, b \in N$, then find the least value of $|a-b|$.

questions.q: Four people sit round a circular table, and each person will roll a normal six sided die once. The probability that no two people sitting next to each other will roll the same number is $\frac{\mathrm{N}}{1296}$. Find the value of $N$.
questions.solution:
$n(S)=6 \cdot 6 \cdot 6 \cdot 6=6^4=1296$ Now A can throw die in 6 ways B can throw only in 5 way D can also throw in 5 ways Suppose C throws the same number as that of A
Hence 'C' can throw only in 1 way $\therefore \quad$ Number of ways $=6 \cdot 5 \cdot 5 \cdot 1=150$ Suppose C throws different number than A A can town in 6 ways $B$ can town in 5 ways Now $C$ will throw different from $A$ and $B$ hence $C$ can throw in 4 ways Now D can not throws what $A$ and $C$ have thrown and therefore can throw in 4 ways. $\therefore \quad$ Number of ways $=6 \cdot 5 \cdot 4 \cdot 4=480$ Total ways $=630$ Ans.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

If we randomly select one of the urns \& draw a ball, then the probability of ball being white is $\frac{p}{q}$ then sum of digits of $p$ is. (Where $p \& q$ are co-prime natural numbers) :

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.solution: Let E : second drawn marble is green. $\begin{aligned} P(E) & =\frac{1}{3}\left[\left\{\frac{1}{4} \cdot 1+\frac{3}{4} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{4}\right)\right\}+\frac{1}{2}\left\{\frac{2}{3}+\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{3}{4}+\frac{1}{4}\right)\right\}+\left\{\frac{3}{4} \cdot \frac{1}{3}+\frac{1}{4} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{3}{4}+\frac{1}{2}\right)\right\}\right] \\ & =\frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{1}{4}+\frac{3}{8} \cdot \frac{3}{4}+\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{2}{3}+\frac{1}{2}\right)+\left(\frac{3}{4} \cdot \frac{1}{3}+\frac{1}{8} \cdot \frac{5}{4}\right)\right]=\frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{1}{4}+\frac{9}{32}+\frac{7}{12}+\frac{13}{32}\right] \\ & =\frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{24+27+56+39}{96}\right]=\frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{146}{96}\right]=\frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{73}{48}\right]=\frac{73}{144} \text { Ans. } \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A pair of students is selected at random from a probability class. The probability that the pair selected will consist of one male and one female student is $\frac{10}{19}$. Find the maximum number of students the class can contain.
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{t}=\mathrm{m}+\mathrm{w}$.
We are given that $\frac{10}{19}=\frac{\mathrm{mw}}{{ }^{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{C}_2}=\frac{2 \mathrm{mw}}{\mathrm{t}(\mathrm{t}-1)}$ $\qquad....(1)$
But $\sqrt{\mathrm{mw}} \leq \frac{\mathrm{m}+\mathrm{w}}{2}$ or $2 \sqrt{\mathrm{mw}} \leq \mathrm{t}$ $\qquad \qquad$ (G.M. $\leq$ A.M.)
or $m w \leq \frac{\mathrm{t}^2}{4}$ $\qquad....(2)$
Substituting $\mathrm{mw}=\left(\frac{10}{19}\right)\left(\frac{\mathrm{t}(\mathrm{t}-1)}{2}\right)$
We have $\left(\frac{5}{19}\right) t(t-1) \leq \frac{t^2}{4}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow 20\left(t^2-t\right) \leq 19 t^2 \Rightarrow 20 t^2-20 t \leq 19 t^2 \text { or } t^2 \leq 20 t \Rightarrow t \leq 20 \\
& \Rightarrow t_{\max }=20
\end{aligned}$
Also equality holds if $\mathrm{m}=\mathrm{w}=10$
$\therefore \quad$ Maximum children in class $=20$ Ans.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
